pack_name: KPMG CRM Technical Specialist Technical Interview Pack
target_role: Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement Technical Consultant / CRM Technical Specialist
notes: Built for high-pressure technical screening and interview rehearsal.
Emphasis on D365 CE, Dataverse, integrations, security, testing, and ALM.
Includes MCQs, deep technical prompts, case studies, and scoring rules.

rubric.0: No usable answer. Incorrect, evasive, or fabricated.
rubric.1: Very weak. Mostly generic buzzwords with poor technical accuracy.
rubric.2: Partial. Some correct concepts but major gaps or missing trade-offs.
rubric.3: Competent. Correct core answer with moderate depth.
rubric.4: Strong. Accurate, specific, and shows sound implementation judgement.
rubric.5: Excellent. Deep, structured, realistic, and defensible under challenge.

global_probe_rules: If candidate answers generically, force a concrete project example.
If candidate chooses a tool, ask why alternatives were rejected.
If candidate says 'best practice', ask how it was implemented and enforced.
If candidate explains design, probe failure modes, supportability, and rollback.
If candidate claims leadership, ask how work was packaged and reviewed.
If candidate names ALM, ask about managed solutions, variables, connection references, approvals, and hotfixes.

questions.question: A requirement states that when a Case is created, routing logic must assign the record before it is committed, block invalid assignments immediately, and avoid asynchronous delay. What is the best implementation choice?

options.C: Cloud flow triggered on row creation

questions.why: Pre-operation synchronous plugins allow validation and value changes before commit inside the transaction.
follow_up_probes: Why not post-operation?
What are the performance risks of synchronous processing?
How would you avoid recursion?

questions.question: Which statement about plugin stages is most accurate?

options.A: Pre-validation occurs inside the main database transaction
options.B: Pre-operation occurs before the core platform operation and is inside the transaction
options.D: Pre-validation is the best place to update large sets of related records

questions.why: Pre-operation executes before the core operation and inside the transaction; post-operation may be synchronous or asynchronous.
follow_up_probes: When would pre-validation be preferable?
Why can too much logic in-transaction be dangerous?

questions.question: Which scenario most strongly favors a plugin over a cloud flow?

options.B: Prevent save when a server-side rule is violated for all entry points

questions.why: Plugins are suitable for transactional, authoritative server-side enforcement.
follow_up_probes: When is cloud flow better?
How do you explain the difference to a functional consultant?

questions.question: A business wants regional users to access only records within their region, executives to see all records, and PII fields to be hidden from most users. Which combination is best?

options.B: Business Units, security roles, and field security profiles

questions.why: Record access is handled with BU/roles while sensitive fields are controlled with field security profiles.
follow_up_probes: When do owner teams help?
What happens when BU design is wrong early?

questions.question: In a controlled enterprise Power Platform delivery model, what is the best default approach?

options.B: Use unmanaged solutions in every environment including production
options.C: Develop unmanaged in lower environments and deploy managed to higher environments
options.D: Avoid solutions and manually recreate configuration

questions.why: This supports controlled promotion, repeatability, and governance.
follow_up_probes: Why not unmanaged in production?
How do environment variables and connection references fit?

questions.question: You need reliable integration between D365 CE and a legacy back-end reachable only over a secured private network. Which architecture is strongest?

options.A: Direct form JavaScript calls to the private system
options.B: Public anonymous webhook to the legacy system
options.C: Azure Function or middleware bridge with secure connectivity, auth, retry, and logging

questions.why: A secure middleware layer isolates risk, controls auth, and supports reliability patterns.
follow_up_probes: How would you secure secrets?
How would you handle timeouts and idempotency?

questions.question: During migration of parent and child records into Dataverse, what is the most critical planning concern?

options.B: Relationship dependency order, alternate keys, and reconciliation

questions.why: Migration depends on entity sequencing, key strategy, and validation.
follow_up_probes: How would you migrate lookups safely?
How do you reconcile source vs target totals?

questions.question: What is the best reason to require pull requests and code review for D365 CE custom code?

options.B: To hide work from functional consultants
options.C: To improve quality, catch defects, and enforce standards

questions.why: PRs improve quality, maintainability, and team consistency.
follow_up_probes: What review checklist would you use?
What should block a merge?

questions.question: Which use case is the strongest fit for JavaScript form scripting rather than a plugin?

options.A: Immediate field visibility changes on the form

questions.why: JavaScript is appropriate for user interaction and form behavior, not authoritative server-side enforcement.
follow_up_probes: When does JavaScript become the wrong choice?
How do you avoid over-customising the client?

questions.question: Which option is generally best for interactive analytical dashboards over D365 data with slicing and drill-down for managers?
questions.why: Power BI is appropriate for analytical dashboards and exploration.
follow_up_probes: When would paginated reports or SSRS still be preferable?
What security concerns would you raise?

questions.question: A plugin updates the same entity in post-operation and starts triggering itself repeatedly. What is the best first defensive measure?

options.B: Use depth checks, filtering attributes, and careful step registration
options.D: Convert all logic to business rules

questions.why: Depth checks and careful registration are standard recursion controls.
follow_up_probes: What is wrong with relying only on depth?
How else would you redesign the pattern?

questions.question: A consultant must choose between business rules, JavaScript, plugin, cloud flow, custom API, and Azure Function. What is the best primary decision principle?

options.A: Use whatever the last project used
options.B: Choose the lowest-complexity tool that still satisfies enforcement scope, transaction timing, and supportability
options.C: Always use plugins because they are more technical
options.D: Always use cloud flows because they are easier

questions.why: The best implementation depends on scope, timing, maintainability, and operational risk.
follow_up_probes: Give one example where the simplest option is wrong.
How do you defend the decision to an architect?

questions.question: What is the biggest operational risk of making hotfixes directly in production without source control and pipeline traceability?

options.A: Users may like the fix too much
options.B: You create environment drift and lose reproducibility across releases

questions.why: Direct prod changes create drift, break auditability, and undermine reproducible releases.
follow_up_probes: How would you recover from an already-drifted production environment?
What emergency process would you define instead?

questions.question: Which statement about connection references and environment variables is most accurate in Power Platform ALM?

options.A: They are optional decoration and have no real deployment value
options.B: They help separate deployable solution assets from environment-specific configuration
options.D: They are only needed for canvas apps

questions.why: They support configuration portability and environment separation.
follow_up_probes: What should never be hardcoded?
Where should secrets live?

questions.question: A managed solution release breaks a critical process in UAT. What is the strongest first response?

options.A: Edit the managed components directly in UAT
options.B: Restore the environment immediately without analysis
options.C: Use your governed rollback/remediation path based on the prior known-good build and deployment logs
options.D: Ask users to keep testing around the issue

questions.why: The right response is controlled rollback or remediation through governed release management.
follow_up_probes: What does rollback actually mean in a Power Platform context?
What evidence must your pipeline retain?

questions.question: What is the strongest testing statement for plugins and integrations in enterprise delivery?

options.B: Unit, integration, regression, and deployment validation all matter
options.D: Testing is optional if the architect reviewed the design

questions.why: Serious delivery requires layered testing, not just UAT.
follow_up_probes: What exactly do you unit test in a plugin?
What should be validated after deployment?

questions.question: Explain the Dataverse plugin execution pipeline in enough detail that a junior developer could register the correct step without guessing.
killer_followups: Give one realistic example for each stage.
When would post-operation synchronous be preferable?

questions.question: Give a decision framework for choosing between business rules, JavaScript, plugin, cloud flow, custom API, and Azure Function.
killer_followups: Apply that framework to skills-based case routing.
Apply it again to SLA breach notifications.

questions.question: Describe an Azure DevOps and ALM model for D365 CE customisations, plugins, flows, apps, and integrations across Dev, Test, UAT, and Prod.
killer_followups: How do you stop prod drift?
How do you handle solution dependencies across teams?
How do you package emergency changes without bypassing governance?

questions.question: What does serious testing look like for a D365 CE implementation with plugins, flows, JavaScript, and integrations?
killer_followups: What exactly do you unit test in a plugin?
What is your minimum release gate before production?

questions.question: What must be included in a technical work package handed to an offshore development team so implementation quality does not collapse?
killer_followups: What would you never leave ambiguous?
How do you review offshore output before integration?

questions.question: When would you choose Power BI, paginated reporting/SSRS, native dashboards, or exported operational reports in a D365 CE programme?
killer_followups: What would you recommend for regulatory reporting?
What would you avoid putting directly on the transactional platform?

questions.question: Design a Dataverse security model for a multi-region service organisation with strict PII controls and audit requirements.
killer_followups: What breaks if the BU hierarchy mirrors the org chart too literally?
How do you handle cross-region collaboration without collapsing boundaries?

questions.question: Walk through a weekend data migration into D365 CE from dirty spreadsheets and line-of-business extracts. What is your sequence and risk control plan?
killer_followups: What would make you reject the weekend cutover?
How do you stop flows/plugins from corrupting bulk load?

questions.scenario: A government agency receives 6,000 citizen queries per day through email, web forms, and a call centre. Current handling is manual. There are repeated SLA breaches, no entitlement enforcement, and agents are unevenly skilled. The business wants automatic routing, escalations, queue balancing, supervisor visibility, and auditability. Assignment logic must be explainable to management.
questions.candidate_task: Design the D365 CE solution architecture. Explain entities, routing, queues, entitlement/SLA handling, whether logic belongs in unified routing, plugin, cloud flow, or both, and how assignment becomes auditable and supportable.
killer_followups: Why not solve everything with Power Automate?
Where exactly would your routing logic execute?
How would you handle agent absence mid-shift?
How would you test routing fairness?
fail_triggers: No distinction between real-time and async processing

questions.scenario: A D365 CE case form must display live customer compliance status from a legacy regulatory platform behind a private network. The legacy system has intermittent downtime and slow response times. The business wants near-real-time lookup but cannot expose the internal platform publicly.
questions.candidate_task: Design the integration architecture and explain security, failure handling, timeouts, caching, logging, support boundaries, and user experience choices.
must_cover: Why direct client-to-legacy calls are risky
killer_followups: How would you stop the form from hanging?
Would you cache results?
How would you protect against duplicate retries?
What would you tell the client if they demanded pure real-time always?
fail_triggers: Direct browser calls to private systems

questions.scenario: A multinational utility wants a Field Service and HSE solution in Dataverse. Site staff should only see records relevant to their operational area. Regional managers see their region. Executives see organisation-wide reporting. Medical and incident sensitivity fields must be restricted to specific roles only. Auditors need read-only access across the estate.
questions.candidate_task: Design the security model. Cover business units, roles, teams, field security, audit needs, and the trade-offs of the model over time.
killer_followups: What breaks if the BU hierarchy mirrors the org chart too literally?
When would access teams be a bad choice?
How would you manage cross-region collaboration?
fail_triggers: No separation of record and field security

questions.scenario: A client has four environments: Dev, Test, UAT, Prod. Multiple consultants are building apps, flows, plugins, and integrations. Releases are failing because configuration differs by environment and hotfixes are being made directly in production. The client wants controlled releases with rollback confidence.
questions.candidate_task: Design the ALM approach. Cover source control, solution strategy, build/release pipeline, environment variables, connection references, approvals, rollback, and governance rules.
killer_followups: How do you roll back a managed solution release?
What belongs in repo and what does not?
How do you stop one consultant from breaking another team's work?

questions.scenario: A non-profit is replacing spreadsheets and paper records with D365 CE. They have 2,500 members, 300 volunteers, 120 donor organisations, duplicates, missing keys, and inconsistent contact information. They want migration over a weekend with minimal downtime and high confidence in the result.
questions.candidate_task: Present a migration strategy from profiling through validation. Include tooling options, sequencing, deduplication, rehearsal cycles, exception handling, and post-load verification.
killer_followups: How do you migrate related records without stable source IDs?
What would make you reject a weekend cutover?
How do you handle plugins/flows that interfere during bulk load?

questions.scenario: A solution architect says: 'Avoid code wherever possible. Use only Power Automate and business rules.' The business then asks for transactional validation, cross-table checks, weighted assignment, guaranteed prevention of invalid saves, and robust supportability under load.
questions.candidate_task: Respond as the technical consultant. Explain where low-code is appropriate, where code is justified, and how you would defend the design to both architect and client.
killer_followups: What is your red line for introducing a plugin?
How do you stop pro-code from turning into vanity engineering?
How would you document these choices for offshore developers?

questions.scenario: A critical production defect is found two hours after a managed solution deployment. It affects case creation for a high-priority business unit. The client wants an immediate fix, but the root cause is still unclear and the release included multiple unrelated changes.
questions.candidate_task: Explain your incident and release response. Cover triage, rollback decision-making, hotfix branching, root-cause isolation, communication, and how to prevent this release pattern from happening again.
killer_followups: When is a full environment restore justified?
How do you prove the hotfix does not reintroduce drift?
What should have been in place before deployment?
fail_triggers: Direct unmanaged edits in production as first choice
No separation between incident response and future governance

questions.scenario: Two parallel teams are delivering changes into the same CE programme: one on core case management and one on field inspections. Their solutions share tables, security roles, and flows. Releases keep colliding and one team's changes frequently break the other.
questions.candidate_task: Design a delivery model that reduces collisions without destroying delivery speed. Cover solution segmentation, ownership boundaries, branching, pipeline safeguards, dependency management, and review practice.
killer_followups: How do you handle truly shared tables?
What do you do when both teams need the same component in the same sprint?
Who arbitrates solution boundaries?
fail_triggers: Single giant unmanaged solution with no ownership

questions.question: Define a complete ALM strategy for a D365 CE programme from developer machine to production support. Speak to repos, solution segmentation, environment strategy, pipelines, approvals, and incident recovery.
killer_followups: Where do unmanaged solutions belong and where do they not?
How do you stop tribal deployment knowledge?

questions.question: Explain the difference between configuration data, customisation, code, secrets, and environment-specific settings in Power Platform ALM. How should each be handled?
expected_points: Key Vault or secure secret storage
killer_followups: What have you seen teams hardcode that they should never hardcode?
What breaks when environment-specific values live in solution logic?

questions.question: How would you implement a safe hotfix process for production while preserving traceability and preventing drift?
killer_followups: When do you choose rollback instead of forward-fix?
How do you stop emergency fixes from bypassing quality permanently?

questions.question: A release includes plugins, flows, app customisations, security role changes, and environment-specific integrations. What release gates would you insist on before production?
expected_points: Solution checker or static checks where relevant
killer_followups: What is your minimum acceptable evidence before deploy?
What would block the release outright?

questions.question: How do you structure solution boundaries when multiple teams work on related apps sharing Dataverse tables and security components?
expected_points: Shared core solution vs feature solutions
killer_followups: When is a shared core solution justified?
How do you stop dependency explosions?

questions.question: Explain rollback in the Power Platform world. Be precise. Do not answer with generic 'restore backup' language only.
expected_points: Rollback is not always simple uninstall
killer_followups: When would uninstalling a managed solution be unsafe?
When does full environment restore become justified?

questions.question: What would you store in Azure DevOps for a D365 CE project, and what would you never store there?
killer_followups: How do you manage service connections and credentials?
Where do deployment runbooks live?

questions.question: How would you detect and remediate production drift when you inherit a project with direct prod edits and undocumented releases?
killer_followups: What if business refuses a freeze window?
How do you communicate risk without sounding dramatic?

auto_fail_conditions: Inventing platform features that do not exist
Confusing client-side UX logic with authoritative server-side enforcement
No understanding of ALM and environment promotion
No security model beyond generic roles
Dogmatic tool selection with no trade-offs
